candidates.description: Supabase Auth plus Postgres/RLS direction for auth, persistence, and tenant isolation boundary.
candidates.why_selected_or_rejected: Selected as the coherent next boundary because it keeps auth, Postgres, RLS, persistence, and tenant isolation direction in one stack evaluation while still requiring a separate implementation pass before any credential or data surface exists.

candidates.description: Clerk for auth plus a separate backend, database, RLS, persistence, and tenant isolation layer.
candidates.why_selected_or_rejected: Kept as a later candidate for a strong auth shell path, but not selected because it leaves the persistence, database, and tenant-isolation boundary split across additional decisions.

candidates.description: Custom backend and authentication implementation owned directly by the project.
candidates.why_selected_or_rejected: Deferred because custom auth would add a security burden too early and would require a broader backend, threat-model, and operational review before any truthful implementation.

candidates.description: Static or client-only auth simulation in the current browser site.
candidates.why_selected_or_rejected: Rejected because client-only credential or account simulation would create a false auth claim and would need to be replaced before real SaaS work.

candidates.description: Continue defining backend contracts and route boundaries while deferring provider selection.
candidates.why_selected_or_rejected: Deferred because the readiness gate already established that backend-first work is required, and this pass can now select a concrete provider boundary for the next non-implementation contract.

selected_stack_boundary_details.server_boundary_direction: server-side authorization boundary required before protected actions
selected_stack_boundary_details.persistence_direction: no persistence until auth and RLS boundary are explicitly implemented
selected_stack_boundary_details.audit_direction: auth and workspace audit events require separate contract before persistence

preconditions_before_real_login_ui: auth provider project and config boundary created
validator updated to distinguish real auth implementation from reserved boundary
preconditions_before_persistence: no customer data until tenant boundary is implemented
preconditions_before_tenant_claims: audit logging for tenant data access defined

smallest_truthful_next_commit.name: §1.2 Backend/Auth Boundary 0.2 — Supabase Project Boundary Contract
smallest_truthful_next_commit.purpose: Define Supabase project/config/environment boundary without credentials, dependencies, auth UI, database schema, or persistence implementation.

claimable_after_this_pass: Supabase full boundary is selected as implementation direction
